message.content:
{
  "competency_groups": [
    {
      "code": "UNIT-I",
      "title": "Relations and Functions",
      "description": "Understanding types of relations, equivalence relations, one-to-one and onto functions.",
      "competencies": [
        "Identify reflexive, symmetric, transitive, and equivalence relations",
        "Determine if a function is one-to-one or onto"
      ],
      "unit_name": "Unit-I: Relations and Functions",
      "chapter_name": ""
    },
    {
      "code": "UNIT-II-1",
      "title": "Inverse Trigonometric Functions",
      "description": "Defining inverse trigonometric functions, understanding their domains, ranges, principal value branches, and graphing.",
      "competencies": [
        "Define inverse trigonometric functions with domain and range",
        "Identify principal value branches of inverse trigonometric functions",
        "Graph inverse trigonometric functions"
      ],
      "unit_name": "Unit-I: Relations and Functions",
      "chapter_name": ""
    },
    {
      "code": "UNIT-II-2",
      "title": "Matrices",
      "description": "Understanding matrix concepts, operations (addition, multiplication, scalar), properties including non-commutativity, transpose, symmetric/skew-symmetric matrices, and invertibility.",
      "competencies": [
        "Classify types of matrices: zero, identity, square, rectangular, symmetric, skew-symmetric",
        "Perform matrix addition, multiplication by a scalar, and matrix multiplication",
        "Demonstrate non-commutativity of matrix multiplication with examples",
        "Find the transpose of a matrix",
        "Determine invertibility of matrices and prove uniqueness of inverse"
      ],
      "unit_name": "Unit-II: Algebra",
      "chapter_name": ""
    },
    {
      "code": "UNIT-II-3",
      "title": "Determinants",
      "description": "Calculating determinants up to 3x3, understanding minors and co-factors, finding area of a triangle, adjoint/inverse matrices, and solving systems of linear equations.",
      "competencies": [
        "Calculate determinant of square matrices (up to order 3)",
        "Compute minors and co-factors",
        "Find the area of a triangle using determinants",
        "Determine adjoint and inverse of a matrix",
        "Analyze consistency, inconsistency, and number of solutions for systems of linear equations",
        "Solve systems of linear equations in two or three variables using matrix inversion"
      ],
      "unit_name": "Unit-II: Algebra",
      "chapter_name": ""
    },
    {
      "code": "UNIT-III-1",
      "title": "Continuity and Differentiability",
      "description": "Understanding continuity, differentiability, chain rule, derivatives of composite/implicit/inverse trigonometric functions, exponential/logarithmic differentiation, parametric forms, and second-order derivatives.",
      "competencies": [
        "Test for continuity and differentiability of a function at a point",
        "Apply the chain rule to differentiate composite functions",
        "Differentiate inverse trigonometric functions (sin^-1 x, cos^-1 x, tan^-1 x)",
        "Derive derivatives of implicit functions",
        "Differentiate exponential and logarithmic functions including logarithmic differentiation",
        "Find derivatives of parametric forms"
      ],
      "unit_name": "Unit-III: Calculus",
      "chapter_name": ""
    },
    {
      "code": "UNIT-III-2",
      "title": "Applications of Derivatives",
      "description": "Applying derivatives to rate of change, increasing/decreasing functions, and finding maxima/minima using first and second derivative tests.",
      "competencies": [
        "Calculate the rate of change of quantities",
        "Determine intervals where a function is increasing or decreasing",
        "Find local maxima and minima using the first derivative test (geometric motivation)",
        "Apply the second derivative test to identify extrema"
      ],
      "unit_name": "Unit-III: Calculus",
      "chapter_name": ""
    },
    {
      "code": "UNIT-III-3",
      "title": "Integrals",
      "description": "Understanding integration as the inverse of differentiation, techniques (substitution, partial fractions, parts), evaluating specific integral forms, Fundamental Theorem of Calculus, and properties of definite integrals.",
      "competencies": [
        "Recognize integration as the inverse process of differentiation",
        "Apply substitution method for integration",
        "Use partial fraction decomposition to integrate rational functions",
        "Integrate by parts",
        "Evaluate standard integral forms involving square roots and quadratic denominators",
        "State and apply the Fundamental Theorem of Calculus (without proof)",
        "Utilize basic properties of definite integrals for evaluation"
      ],
      "unit_name": "Unit-III: Calculus",
      "chapter_name": ""
    },
    {
      "code": "UNIT-III-4",
      "title": "Application of Integrals",
      "description": "Calculating the area under simple curves including lines, circles, parabolas, and ellipses in standard form.",
      "competencies": [
        "Calculate the area bounded by a line or circle using integration",
        "Determine areas enclosed by parabolas and ellipses (standard forms only)"
      ],
      "unit_name": "Unit-III: Calculus",
      "chapter_name": ""
    },
    {
      "code": "UNIT-III-5",
      "title": "Differential Equations",
      "description": "Defining differential equations, understanding order and degree, finding general/particular solutions, solving by separation of variables, homogeneous equations, and linear forms.",
      "competencies": [
        "Identify the order and degree of a differential equation",
        "Differentiate between general and particular solutions",
        "Solve first-order differential equations using method of separation of variables",
        "Solve homogeneous differential equations of first order and first degree",
        "Solve linear differential equations of type dy/dx + py = q (p, q functions of x or constants)",
        "Solve linear differential equations of type dx/dy + px = q (p, q functions of y or constants)"
      ],
      "unit_name": "Unit-III: Calculus",
      "chapter_name": ""
    },
    {
      "code": "UNIT-IV-1",
      "title": "Vectors",
      "description": "Understanding vectors and scalars, magnitude/direction, types of vectors (equal, unit, zero, parallel, collinear), position vectors, components, vector operations (addition, scalar multiplication), dot product, and cross product.",
      "competencies": [
        "Distinguish between vectors and scalars",
        "Calculate magnitude and direction cosines/ratios of a vector",
        "Classify types of vectors: equal, unit, zero, parallel, collinear",
        "Find position vectors and components of vectors",
        "Perform addition of vectors and multiplication by a scalar",
        "Apply properties of dot product (scalar) and cross product (vector)"
      ],
      "unit_name": "Unit-IV: Vectors and Three-dimensional Geometry",
      "chapter_name": ""
    },
    {
      "code": "UNIT-IV-2",
      "title": "Three-Dimensional Geometry",
      "description": "Finding direction cosines/ratios of lines, equations of lines (Cartesian/Vector), skew lines, shortest distance between lines, and angles between lines.",
      "competencies": [
        "Determine direction cosines and ratios for a line joining two points",
        "Formulate Cartesian and vector equations of a line in 3D space",
        "Identify skew lines"
      ],
      "unit_name": "Unit-IV: Vectors and Three-dimensional Geometry",
      "chapter_name": ""
    },
    {
      "code": "UNIT-V",
      "title": "Linear Programming Problem",
      "description": "Understanding terminology (constraints, objective function), graphical method for 2-variable problems, feasible/infeasible regions, and optimal solutions.",
      "competencies": [
        "Define constraints, objective functions, optimization, feasible, infeasible, and optimal solutions"
      ],
      "unit_name": "Unit-V: Linear Programming Problem",
      "chapter_name": ""
    },
    {
      "code": "UNIT-VI",
      "title": "Probability",
      "description": "Understanding conditional probability, multiplication theorem, independent events, total probability, and Bayes' theorem.",
      "competencies": [
        "Calculate conditional probabilities"
      ],
      "unit_name": "Unit-VI: Probability",
      "chapter_name": ""
    }
  ]
}
